title: ledum palustre f. yesoense
intro: Ledum palustre f. yesoense, also known as Labrador tea, is a subspecies of the Labrador tea plant (Ledum palustre), a type of evergreen shrub native to the Arctic and subarctic regions. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and antibacterial qualities, which can help to prevent infection and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Labrador tea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties that can aid in the treatment of various ailments. Labrador tea can be prepared as a tea by infusing the leaves in hot water, or as a tincture, which can be used to create topical applications and other herbal preparations.
